sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.94 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: The Gruenberg--Kegel graph (or the prime graph)$GK(G)$of a finite group~$G$is a labelled graph with vertex set consisting of all prime divisors of the order of$G$in which different vertices$p$and$q$are adjacent if and only if$G$contains an element of order~$pq$. Let$(G)$denote the abstract graph obtained from$GK(G)$by removing all labels. A finite group$G$is said to be if there are no finite group...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
